articles.title: The Filing Clock: Hour-of-Day Patterns in USPTO Electronic Submissions
articles.content:
专利律师究竟何时提交申请？既不是一早刚上班，也不是工作日临近结束时匆匆提交。在2025年5月至6月约47.5万份美国专利商标局电子申请中，申请量从上午开始稳步攀升，在东部时间下午3点达到峰值，随后在晚间逐渐回落。四分之一的申请在下午5点之后提交。周末提交量不足2%。

此处的计时以弗吉尼亚州为准。根据《联邦法规汇编》第37编第1.6(a)(4)条，通过专利中心提交的任何文件，其收到日期以美国专利商标局接收时的东部时间为准。一位加州律师若在太平洋时间晚上10点点击提交，其文件将获得次日作为提交日期。
articles.summary:
by Dennis Crouch

When do patent attorneys actually file? Not first thing in the morning, and not in a rush at the end of the business day. Across roughly 475,000 USPTO electronic filings May-June 2025, volume climbs steadily through the morning, peaks in the 3:00 p.m. hour Eastern, and then runs down a long evening tail. A quarter of everything arrives after 5:00 p.m.  Weekends account for under 2%.

The clock here sits in Virginia. Under 37 C.F.R. § 1.6(a)(4), the receipt date of anything submitted through Patent Center is the Eastern Time date when the USPTO received it. A California attorney who clicks submit at 10:00 p.m. Pacific gets the next day's filing date.

To continue reading, become a Patently-O member. Already a member? Simply log in to access the full post.
